a6ce8c62-496e-4503-88e9-46dfaee4deb8.text: Our members are committed hepatologists, researchers, surgeons, scientists, trainees and other health care professionals, who are dedicated to advancing the science of liver disease, making AASLD the leader in education, advocacy, and career development.

35a4b76e-d1ce-4404-8a11-48aed39debfe.text: What You’ll Need to Become a Member Gather your files—a current CV or, if you are applying as a Trainee, a letter from your Program Director Create an account Once you’re logged in, click “membership application” to start the process Upload your CV or Trainee information Ready to Become a Member? [Join AASLD](https://my.aasld.org/join) After you have submitted an application, please allow for up to two weeks for your membership to be finalized.

032c5caf-8503-4654-b9c2-2679289db1b8.text: Renew your AASLD membership today to stay informed on the latest research and developments in hepatology, network with thought leaders and colleagues in the field, advance your career, and save money through exclusive member discounts on AASLD meetings and events. Log in to your AASLD account to verify your membership status, renew your membership, and/or update your member profile with current information. Or, if you have your dues invoice and membership ID number, find your invoice and pay online.

d524c137-9c9d-43a2-9820-eb4d4a453d4f.text: Steering Committee positions can be occupied by AASLD membership categories that are in good standing and are SIG members. Chair The Chair plays a crucial leadership role within the SIG, overseeing the steering committee and ensuring effective governance and decision-making.

63784e8e-12aa-4fd5-9c57-417c09b5028d.text: Key responsibilities include: Leadership: The Chair is the primary leader of the SIG steering committee, guiding the committee’s function to achieve the goals of the SIG membership and ensuring that it remains aligned with the strategic goals of the SIG and the organization.

5b1f1ad1-db67-49e5-bf2c-19090a28e2b6.text: Collaboration with the Governing Board: The Chair may be requested to support additional tasks from the AASLD Governing Board and will ensure that the committee’s work is aligned with the broader organizational goals, and periodic updates are reported back to the Board. Quarterly Calls: The Chair organizes and leads quarterly steering committee calls, collaborating with a staff representative to set the agenda and ensuring productive discussions.

199ddb06-15a3-4b33-b834-3d513258b941.text: The Chair’s role is critical in ensuring the SIG’s success by p roviding leadership, fostering collaboration, and aligning the steering committee’s work with the organization’s overarching goals. Following their term, chairs will be invited to serve as a member of the SIG Advisory Working Group. Vice-Chair The Vice-Chair plays a vital support role within the SIG, working closely with and assisting the Chair in various leadership and administrative functions.

5b492d0b-9a4b-457a-aa98-471105273654.text: They are structured to provide interaction among members in a wide range of areas. Committee members deal with the issues and challenges affecting their area of expertise within the field, and help devise strategies, plans, and programs as directed by the Governing Board and the AASLD strategic plan. Each committee is comprised of current AASLD members and a Governing Board liaison as well as an AASLD staff member who serves as the liaison to the committee in the role of a staff advisor.
